examples.en: Investors agreed to bankroll this top-notch line, but there were stumbling blocks.
To cut expenditure they must build cheaply, yet despite people's appetite for speed, the project faced lengthy delays.

examples.en: Until the firm can sway officials to see the whole picture, no one can take a spin.
The law set down across the state bans anything that could place riders in harm's way if the pod cannot decelerate in time.
The law set down across the state bans anything that could place riders in harm's way if the pod cannot decelerate in time.
To cut expenditure they must build cheaply, yet despite people's appetite for speed, the project faced lengthy delays.

Until the company can persuade local governments to authorise the project, the train of the future stays on the drawing board.
examples.linking: Until the company can persuade local governments to‿authorise the project, the train‿of the future stays‿on the drawing board.
tips: 連結: train‿of, stays‿on=ステイゾン

強勢: COM·pany, per·SUADE, AU·thorise, PRO·ject, DRAW·ing BOARD
注意音: R(persuade/authorise/project/train/board), TH(the)

The pod can zip between cities, a plan that sounds like something from the pages of a novel.
The law set down across the state bans anything that could place riders in harm's way if the pod cannot decelerate in time.

items.front: had + 過去分詞（過去完了）
items.meaning: 過去のある時点より前に完了した動作・状態を表す

examples.en: By 1985, previous attempts had all failed.
examples.jp: 1985年までに、以前の試みは全て失敗していた。

pron.tts: By 1985, previous attempts had all failed.

items.meaning: evidence/information/advice/research/progress は不可算。複数形・a/many は不可。much/a lot of を使う

examples.en: There is a lot of evidence for this.
examples.jp: これには多くの証拠がある。（×many evidences）

pron.tts: There is a lot of evidence for this.

items.front: move の用法（移住 vs 移動）
items.meaning: move は『永続的に住む場所を移す』時に使う。日常の移動には go を使う

examples.en: I go to work by train.
examples.jp: 電車で通勤します。（×I move to work by train）

pron.tts: I go to work by train.
